title: Risk assessment stays memory-first
summary: Kage risk borrows repo-intelligence signals without changing Kage's core: combine the existing code graph with local git history before edits.
body:
Kage risk should borrow repo-intelligence signals from tools like Repowise by combining the existing code graph with local git history: dependents, impact surface, churn, ownership, co-change partners, and test gaps. Keep it local-first and memory-adjacent rather than replacing Kage with a generic static map.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp
